name: NetBird
pricing_notes: Free: 5 users/peers; Team $5/user/mo; Enterprise custom; self-hosted free

auth.notes: PAT (personal access token) for management API; device enrollment via OIDC or PAT

performance.notes: WireGuard overlay; peer-to-peer when possible; relay fallback for NAT traversal

agent_readiness.notes: WireGuard-based overlay network for zero-trust access. Management API for programmatic peer management, network policies, DNS. Self-hostable or NetBird Cloud. Good for connecting distributed agent infrastructure securely.

gotchas: Go-primary (Python/JS clients via REST management API)
Initial setup requires agent installation on each peer
Management API less mature than Tailscale
Not suitable for end-user consumer VPN use cases
best_when: Connecting distributed AI agent infrastructure securely across clouds
Tailscale/ZeroTier alternative for teams wanting self-hosted zero-trust network
Mesh VPN for micro-services needing encrypted service-to-service comms
Organizations with regulatory requirements for self-hosted network infrastructure
avoid_when: Large scale requiring enterprise support (Tailscale more mature)
summary: NetBird is an open-source WireGuard-based overlay network for zero-trust peer-to-peer connectivity. Management API for programmatic network configuration. Self-hostable (free) or NetBird Cloud. Best for distributed agent infrastructure needing secure encrypted connectivity across clouds without complex VPN setup. Tailscale alternative with open-source self-hosting option.
